metadata.operator_pasted_jtnb_subtype: Hard reject (no invitation to resubmit; rejection reason verbatim: 'After consideration, we feel that the content of the manuscript does not meet the standards expected for publication in the journal.')
metadata.venue_labelling_drift_correction: Per 047 D3, P11 was actually JTNB-only at SICF authoring time (Math.Comp desk-rejected 2026-04-28; resubmitted same day to JTNB-2400). Each option below is scored under both the original Math.Comp framing (counterfactual) and the post-correction JTNB framing (actual).

axis_1.name: manuscript_substance_preservation_pct
axis_1.definition: Percentage of original P11 v1 (F(2,4) Base Case) manuscript claims surviving the option without semantic weakening. Numerical content (Theorem 4.1 classification table, F(2,4) base-case enumeration, Möbius identification numerical evidence) counts; semantic strength of named theorems counts (e.g., 'Theorem 5.3 (transcendence)' demoted to 'Conjecture 5.3*' is a substance reduction).
axis_1.estimation_basis: qualitative judgment grounded in the four-fatals + #18 inventory at sicf_options_verbatim.md §C; not a measured metric

axis_2.name: time_to_resubmit_days
axis_2.definition: Wall-clock days from option-execution start to a resubmission-ready manuscript at a target venue (any venue), excluding subsequent referee turnaround.
axis_2.estimation_basis: verbatim cost figures from sicf_options_verbatim.md §A where given; SICF-roadmap-execution estimates otherwise

axis_3.name: aeal_chain_integrity
axis_3.definition: Whether the option requires re-running any computational claim already entered in claims.jsonl. Reported as {rerun_required: bool, rerun_cpu_hr: float, integrity_pct: int}. integrity_pct = 100 if no rerun; lower if any AEAL line needs reverification.
axis_3.estimation_basis: inspection of which fatals touch numerical-versus-narrative content

options.name: No-op (wait for referee verdict)
options.verbatim_summary: If Math of Comp returns Reject, the SICF analysis becomes the resubmission roadmap (4 fatal must-fix items). Cost: zero. Risk: editor sees the same issues independently.

scoring_pre_verdict_counterfactual.axis_2_note: wait-state; no resubmission action initiated until verdict lands

scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEG.axis_2_note: DOMINATED by verdict landing 2026-05-06; the wait-state has zero residual action surface (verdict is in)

options.name: Voluntary corrigendum
options.verbatim_summary: Submit a 1-2 page corrigendum to Math of Comp addressing #18 (2-family arithmetic discrepancy) since that's the one hard error not requiring a full rewrite. Cost: ~1 day. Risk: signals ongoing instability.

axis_3.rerun_scope: recount Theorem 4.1 partition (26 vs 24+0+0); minor PSLQ replays at dps=500

scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEG.axis_2_note: DOMINATED by hard reject. A corrigendum requires an accepted-or-published host-journal version to attach to; JTNB rejection without acceptance leaves no editorial relationship to leverage.

options.name: Withdrawal + restructure (resubmit)
options.verbatim_summary: Withdraw, address #1-#4 by reframing the theorem statements as 'T1-relative' and the partition as 'conditional on basis-completeness', resubmit. Cost: 2-3 weeks. Risk: 1 month of review queue restart.

scoring_pre_verdict_counterfactual.axis_1_note: fatals #1-#3 reframe to T1-relative + algorithm-conditional (no semantic loss in numerical content; loss is in the rhetorical strength of Theorem 4.1's 'completeness' claim and Theorem 5.3's transcendence-proof status). Numerical classification table (513,387/400,093/24/etc.) preserved verbatim. Möbius identification (Prop 5.2) demoted from theorem-grade evidence to conjecture-grade; Theorem 5.3 (transcendence of K) demoted to Conjecture 5.3* or removed from base case. ~80% of original manuscript's substance preserved; ~20% lost in named-theorem demotion + scope qualification.

axis_3.rerun_scope: reconcile #18 arithmetic (no new computation, recount); re-anchor SHAs after restructure; no large sweeps re-run

scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEG.axis_1_note: Same as pre-verdict scoring on the manuscript-content axis. The 'withdraw' verb in the option label is moot under hard reject (rejection already removes from JTNB); only the 'restructure + resubmit' core survives. Resubmission target shifts from 'JTNB R1' to 'a different venue' (originality-friendly because restructured), but axis_1 (substance preservation) is unaffected by venue change.
scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEG.axis_2_note: Restructure cost (2-3 weeks) preserved; the 1-month-review-queue-restart-risk note from sicf_options_verbatim.md §A is now realised in the form of a fresh-venue review cycle (typical 3-6 mo) rather than JTNB queue reset, but that downstream cost is outside axis_2's option-execution-window scope.

axis_3.rerun_scope: reconcile #18 arithmetic (no new computation, recount); re-anchor SHAs after restructure; no large sweeps re-run

options.name: Hold for JTNB-2400 leverage (cross-venue strategy)
options.verbatim_summary: If JTNB referee raises any of the 4 SICF fatals, address all four in one R1 response; if Math of Comp referee raises them independently, the JTNB pipeline is harder to defend. Synth strategic call.

scoring_pre_verdict_counterfactual.axis_2_note: no-action posture; conditional on referee behaviour at either venue
scoring_pre_verdict_counterfactual.venue_labelling_drift_note: Already degenerate at SICF authoring time per 047 D3: there was no parallel Math.Comp submission to leverage against; P11 was JTNB-only after 2026-04-28 desk-rejection. Under D3 correction this option reduces to 'wait for JTNB referee; absorb fatals into JTNB R&R if/when raised', which is structurally equivalent to Option 1.

scoring_post_verdict_actual_RECEIVED_NEG.axis_2_note: DOMINATED by hard reject. JTNB door closed; no leverage to hold. Under venue-drift correction (D3), the option is degenerate to start with; under hard reject it is fully dominated.

decision_summary.selection_basis: FORCED_BY_ELIMINATION (3 of 4 dominated by post-2026-05-06 hard-reject reality; Option 3 is the only LIVE option)

binding_window_note.rationale: JTNB instructions-to-authors page (https://jtnb.centre-mersenne.org/page/instructions-to-authors/) returns blank/empty content via webfetch 2026-05-06; no published 'withdraw-and-resubmit window' policy is exposed. The only published JTNB constraint is the originality requirement: 'publishes original papers on number theory and related topics (not published elsewhere)' (jtnb.centre-mersenne.org root page, fetched 2026-05-06). Under hard reject, the relay-049 'JTNB withdraw-and-resubmit window narrowing' framing (drafted at AWAITED state) does not map to a live journal-side window. The functional binding window is operator-conceptual: SICF-roadmap-freshness while four-fatals-fix decisions are in working memory.
binding_window_note.estimate_rationale: 30 days is the conservative SICF-roadmap-freshness window (4 fatals enumerated 2026-05-05; restructure dispatch within 30 days keeps fatal-fix mental model intact and avoids needing a fresh SICF re-run before resubmission). This is a JUDGMENT CALL, not a derived deadline.
